title: HGQ: High Granularity Quantization for Real-time Neural Networks on FPGAs
venue: ACM/SIGDA International Symposium on Field Programmable Gate Arrays
application_domain_or_eda_subdomain: FPGA deployment; low-latency neural network inference
ml_techniques: quantization-aware training; differentiable bit-width learning
main_problem_addressed: Reduce FPGA resource consumption and inference latency for real-time neural networks while preserving accuracy.
primary_contribution_summary: Introduces HGQ, a differentiable quantization-aware training method that learns bit-widths at arbitrary granularity (up to per-parameter) and jointly optimizes accuracy and FPGA resource usage; provides an open-source software-hardware co-design workflow integrated with hls4ml and da4ml.
lifecycle_activities_supported: training; hardware-aware optimization; deployment
data_management_support: none explicit
training_support: yes; differentiable QAT with learnable bit-widths and resource-aware regularization
validation_testing_support: experimental FPGA-oriented evaluation on benchmark tasks
deployment_support: yes; export/deployment through hls4ml and da4ml backends
monitoring_feedback_loop: no
ci_cd_or_automation_support: limited; automated mixed-precision search during training
integration_pattern: ML training tightly coupled with FPGA cost/resource model and deployment backends
tools_frameworks_platforms: HGQ; hls4ml; da4ml; Vivado/Vitis
reported_limitations: Best suited to compact models that can fit on a single FPGA with microsecond-level latency constraints; LUT-based approaches may be more resource-efficient for very small networks.
reported_threats_or_constraints: Ultra-low latency imposes hard resource constraints; current LUT-based approaches have scalability/portability limitations; framework targets FPGA-friendly fixed-point arithmetic.
gap_tags_initial: resource-aware training; mixed-precision; FPGA deployment; no full MLOps lifecycle
why_relevant_for_our_positioning: Relevant as an explicit HW/SW co-design workflow linking model training and FPGA deployment, but focused on low-latency inference optimization rather than end-to-end EDA/MLOps pipelines.
notes_for_manual_fulltext_reading: Capture exact evaluation tasks, FPGA platforms, and whether the resource estimator can generalize across devices.

title: Special Sessions - Hardware-Software Co-Design for Machine Learning Systems Made Open-Source

lifecycle_activities_supported: model development; compiler transformation; synthesis; design-space exploration; security verification
data_management_support: not central
training_support: mentions hardware-aware training/optimization
validation_testing_support: discussion/position paper, not deep empirical validation
monitoring_feedback_loop: mentions real-time monitoring and confidence-aware inference
ci_cd_or_automation_support: yes; advocates unified open-source toolchains and early co-design
integration_pattern: connect ML frameworks (PyTorch/TensorFlow) with compilers, open ISA/IP, EDA synthesis, security, and deployment contexts

why_relevant_for_our_positioning: Highly relevant for gap framing because it explicitly argues for integrated, open, lifecycle-aware HW/SW co-design stacks but remains mostly conceptual.

title: The State of Simulation Frameworks for Evaluating Emerging LLM Accelerators

why_relevant_for_our_positioning: Relevant as an ML-enhanced compiler/toolchain optimization workflow, but it focuses on autotuning a deep-learning accelerator rather than end-to-end DevOps for HW/SW co-design of embedded systems.

title: MetaML-Pro: Cross-Stage Design Flow Automation for Efficient Deep Learning Acceleration
